CREATE TABLE test_nvarchar (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value NVARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_nvarchar (id, value) VALUES (1, 'TestValue');
INSERT INTO test_nvarchar (id, value) VALUES (2, 'A' || REPEAT('B', 253));
INSERT INTO test_nvarchar (id, value) VALUES (3, 'C' || REPEAT('D', 254));
SELECT * FROM test_nvarchar WHERE LENGTH(value) <= 255;
SELECT * FROM test_nvarchar WHERE value LIKE 'Test%';
INSERT INTO test_nvarchar (id, value) VALUES (4, NULL);
CREATE TABLE test_join (id INT PRIMARY KEY, foreign_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (foreign_id) REFERENCES test_nvarchar(id));
INSERT INTO test_join (id, foreign_id) VALUES (1, 1);
INSERT INTO test_join (id, foreign_id) VALUES (2, 2);
SELECT a.id, a.value, b.foreign_id FROM test_nvarchar a INNER JOIN test_join b ON a.id = b.foreign_id;
SELECT a.id, COUNT(b.id) FROM test_nvarchar a LEFT JOIN test_join b ON a.id = b.foreign_id GROUP BY a.id;
SELECT * FROM test_nvarchar WHERE value IN (SELECT value FROM test_nvarchar WHERE id < 3);
SELECT DISTINCT value FROM test_nvarchar WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
UPDATE test_nvarchar SET value = 'UpdatedValue' WHERE id = 1;
DELETE FROM test_nvarchar WHERE id IN (SELECT foreign_id FROM test_join WHERE id = 1);
CREATE TABLE complex_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, description NVARCHAR(500), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW());
INSERT INTO complex_data (id, description) VALUES (1, 'Record for Testing the NVARCHAR Functionality');
SELECT id, COUNT(value) FROM test_nvarchar GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(value) > 0;
SELECT a.id, LENGTH(a.value) AS length_value FROM test_nvarchar a ORDER BY length_value DESC;
